task_completion.description: Search for the patient 'Bernhard Morar' in the medical system.

task_completion.description: Open the patient dashboard for Bernhard Morar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard or chart for 'Bernhard Morar' is displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate the active 'Facility Visit' started today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An active visit labeled 'Facility Visit' with today's date is visible in the visits section of the patient's dashboard.

task_completion.description: Initiate the action to delete or void the erroneous facility visit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu option, button, or link for deleting, voiding, or ending the specific 'Facility Visit' is clicked or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Confirm the deletion or voiding of the visit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation modal/dialog for deleting or voiding the visit is interacted with, or a success toast notification appears indicating the visit was removed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the visit is no longer active on the patient's dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient's dashboard is shown again, and the previously active 'Facility Visit' from today is no longer listed under active or recent visits.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the web-based medical system interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser interface being navigated, rather than terminal commands or API scripts being executed.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual medical application and did not fake the search in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search and patient dashboard are within the genuine medical record system (e.g., OpenMRS), not a generic text editor or unrelated application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to visually fake the deletion of the visit.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are open in the screenshots to manipulate the DOM.
